<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishikuma-kofun]][[en:Nishikuma Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishikuma-kofun]][[en:Nishikuma Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nishikuma-kofun]][[en:Nishikuma Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishikuma Kofun | native_name =西隈古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishikuma-kofun stone wall.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishikuma Kofun | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishikuma Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|19|39|N|130|17|36|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Saga, Saga|Saga, Saga]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|39|N|130|17|36|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nishikuma Kofun'''|西隈古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kinryumachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saga, Saga|Saga]], {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217709|title=西隈古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishikuma Kofun is located on a plateau at the southern foot of the {{ill|Mount Sefuri|en|Mount Sefuri|WD=Q8528436|lt=Sefuri mountain range|1=Mount Sefuri}}, in the northern part of the Saga Plain. It is a two-tiered circular {{nihono|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about four meters, built in two tiers, and the presence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and figurative and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been confirmed. The surrounding area of the tumulus has been eroded by urban encroachment and farmland.The horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is orientated to the west. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.5 meters wide, and 17 meters high, and the surrounding walls are topped with a waist stone at the base, with slightly flattened blocks of stone piled on top of that. Outside the entrance, there is a vestibule side wall that is 2.8 meters long and 1.1 to 1.4 meters wide. All of the stones used are {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}}, and the interior of the burial chamber is painted with vermilion pigment. The stone sarcophagus inside the burial chamber is made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}}, and is placed in the direction of the chamber's main axis. It is 2 meters long, 1.1 meters wide, and 1.3 meters high, and is made of four stone slabs combined, with no bottom stone.The sarcophagus lid has a hipped roof, and there are two ring-shaped rope hanging protrusions on each of its long sides. The edges of the sarcophagus body and lid facing the entrance are engraved with circular and consecutive triangular patterns that leave compass marks, and the parts other than the patterns are painted with vermillion pigment. This tomb had previously been the victim of grave robbery, and only a few {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as armor fragments and iron arrowheads have been excavated. However, judging from the structure of the burial chamber and sarcophagus, it is estimated to have been built around the end of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 8.2 kilometers north of {{ill|Saga Station|en|Saga Station|WD=Q1042665|lt=Saga Station|1=Saga Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|en|Nagasaki Main Line|WD=Q1078117|lt=Nagasaki Main Line|1=Nagasaki Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Nishikuma-kofun}} *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031658/index.html Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishikuma-kofun]][[en:Nishikuma Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nishikuma-kofun]][[en:Nishikuma Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nishikuma Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Nishikuma Kofun | native_name =西隈古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nishikuma-kofun stone wall.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nishikuma Kofun | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nishikuma Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|19|39|N|130|17|36|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Saga, Saga|Saga, Saga]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|39|N|130|17|36|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nishikuma Kofun'''|西隈古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kinryumachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saga, Saga|Saga]], {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217709|title=西隈古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishikuma Kofun is located on a plateau at the southern foot of the {{ill|Mount Sefuri|en|Mount Sefuri|WD=Q8528436|lt=Sefuri mountain range|1=Mount Sefuri}}, in the northern part of the Saga Plain. It is a two-tiered circular {{nihono|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of about 30 meters and a height of about four meters, built in two tiers, and the presence of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and figurative and cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been confirmed. The surrounding area of the tumulus has been eroded by urban encroachment and farmland.The horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is orientated to the west. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.5 meters wide, and 17 meters high, and the surrounding walls are topped with a waist stone at the base, with slightly flattened blocks of stone piled on top of that. Outside the entrance, there is a vestibule side wall that is 2.8 meters long and 1.1 to 1.4 meters wide. All of the stones used are {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}}, and the interior of the burial chamber is painted with vermilion pigment. The stone sarcophagus inside the burial chamber is made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}}, and is placed in the direction of the chamber's main axis. It is 2 meters long, 1.1 meters wide, and 1.3 meters high, and is made of four stone slabs combined, with no bottom stone.The sarcophagus lid has a hipped roof, and there are two ring-shaped rope hanging protrusions on each of its long sides. The edges of the sarcophagus body and lid facing the entrance are engraved with circular and consecutive triangular patterns that leave compass marks, and the parts other than the patterns are painted with vermillion pigment. This tomb had previously been the victim of grave robbery, and only a few {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as armor fragments and iron arrowheads have been excavated. However, judging from the structure of the burial chamber and sarcophagus, it is estimated to have been built around the end of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 8.2 kilometers north of {{ill|Saga Station|en|Saga Station|WD=Q1042665|lt=Saga Station|1=Saga Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|en|Nagasaki Main Line|WD=Q1078117|lt=Nagasaki Main Line|1=Nagasaki Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
